input:
Indeed, he was to become one of the most distinguished commanders the world had ever known. Khālid reports in detail how he began to think about Islam and the process of his conversion. He says that at first he started to reflect on his past attitude towards Islam: I fought all those battles against Muhammad. Every time I felt that all my efforts were to no avail. I was certain that Muhammad would eventually be the winner. When the Prophet came and encamped at al-Ḥudaybiyah, I commanded a detachment of horsemen from among the idolaters until we met the Prophet and his companions at ʿAsafān.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 32: A Second Trip to Makkah

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 32: A Second Trip to Makkah

characters.name: Khālid ibn al-Walīd
characters.appearance: Mid 30s, fair skin, athletic build, tall. Thick black beard, neatly kept. Wearing a dark tan leather tunic, off-white linen shirt underneath, dark brown woolen cloak fastened at the shoulder with a bronze brooch. Dark brown leather boots. Confident, direct gaze.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: It's never too late to change and make amends for past mistakes, and to seek guidance and truth.
output.narrative_text: Khālid ibn al-Walīd began to think about Islam when he saw the Prophet's camp at al-Ḥudaybiyah. He remembered all the battles he had fought against the Prophet, but now he was starting to question his past actions.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, standing in the distance, with Khālid ibn al-Walīd walking towards him. Scene depicts a moment of introspection and questioning, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. A desert landscape with scattered palm trees, under a clear blue sky with a few white clouds. In the distance, a man in a white thobe and green cloak, white turban, is standing, backlit by the sun. Closer to the viewer, a young Arab man with fair skin, thick black beard, dark tan leather tunic, off-white shirt, and dark brown woolen cloak, is walking towards the Prophet, looking thoughtful. Simple desert terrain, distant mountains, medium shot, eye level. Warm, sunny palette. No modern objects.
